comments.body: CodeAnt AI is reviewing your PR.

## Reviewer's Guide Implements zero-allocation struct-based signal broadcasting, hardens flatten/stop-sync/lifecycle safety paths to avoid fail-fast behavior, and makes IPC broadcasting resilient by isolating per-client failures while preserving fleet-wide operations. #### Sequence diagram for SIMA flatten fallback and non-fail-fast behavior ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Strategy participant SIMA_Flatten participant NT8_Engine Strategy->>SIMA_Flatten: FlattenAllApexAccounts() SIMA_Flatten->>NT8_Engine: TriggerCustomEvent(PumpFlattenOps, null) alt InvalidOperationException [message contains TriggerCustomEvent] SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: isFlattenRunning = false SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: Print("[FLATTEN] WARNING: TriggerCustomEvent failed") else Exception SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: isFlattenRunning = false SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: while(_pendingFlattenOps.TryDequeue(out item)) drainedOps.Add(item) SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: Print("[FLATTEN] CRITICAL: Unexpected error in FlattenAllApexAccounts") loop drainedOps SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_CancelOrders(workItem, acct) SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: ProcessFlattenWorkItem_ClosePositions(workItem, acct) SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: SetExpectedPositionLocked(ExpKey(acct.Name), 0) SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: Print("[FLATTEN] Fallback flatten succeeded") end SIMA_Flatten->>SIMA_Flatten: // Do NOT rethrow end note over SIMA_Flatten: Remaining fleet accounts continue to flatten despite failures ``` #### Sequence diagram for stop-sync emergency flatten on stop creation failure ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Strategy participant StopSync participant NT8_Engine Strategy->>StopSync: UpdateStopQuantity(entryName, pos) StopSync->>StopSync: CreateNewStopOrder(entryName, quantity, stopPrice, direction, isRecovery) StopSync->>NT8_Engine: SubmitOrderUnmanaged / CreateOrder alt InvalidOperationException [message contains SubmitOrderUnmanaged or CreateOrder] StopSync->>StopSync: Print("WARNING CreateNewStopOrder (known quirk)") else Exception StopSync->>StopSync: Print("CRITICAL CreateNewStopOrder: ex.ToString()") StopSync->>StopSync: Print("Attempting emergency flatten for entryName...") StopSync->>StopSync: FlattenPositionByName(entryName) opt FlattenPositionByName throws StopSync->>StopSync: Print("CRITICAL: Emergency flatten also failed") end StopSync->>StopSync: // Do NOT rethrow end note over StopSync: Position safety favored over fail-fast behavior ``` ### File-Level Changes | Change | Details | Files | | ------ | ------- | ----- | | Harden flatten pipeline to avoid fail-fast behavior while adding targeted handling for known NT8 quirks and a synchronous fallback path. |
  • In FlattenAllApexAccounts, add specific handling for known TriggerCustomEvent InvalidOperationException, release the isFlattenRunning guard, and log warnings instead of failing.
  • On unexpected flatten errors, drain the pending flatten queue and perform best-effort synchronous fallback flatten per account with detailed logging, without rethrowing.
  • In PumpFlattenOps, split catch blocks into known order-operation InvalidOperationException (log and continue) and general exceptions (log critical, do not rethrow) while always chaining the next operation.
  • In ChainNextFlattenOp and ClosePositionsOnlyApexAccounts, treat TriggerCustomEvent InvalidOperationException as a known quirk, log and clear the running flag, and avoid rethrowing on unexpected errors.
  • In EmergencyFlattenSingleFleetAccount, distinguish known order-operation InvalidOperationException from unexpected errors, logging critical details without rethrowing to keep processing remaining accounts.
| `src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs` | | Maintain struct-based, zero-allocation signal events and clarify Jane Street alignment and validation semantics in the broadcaster. |
  • Keep signal DTOs as structs and events as Action to preserve zero-allocation behavior while updating XML comments to explicitly reference Jane Street Deviation #1.
  • Clarify SafeInvoke documentation to note the reversion to Action-based struct events.
  • Simplify comments on broadcast methods from "struct validation" to generic "validation" while preserving argument checks for required fields.
| `src/SignalBroadcaster.cs` | | Relax fail-fast behavior in stop synchronization to prioritize position safety, with explicit handling of known NinjaTrader order quirks and emergency flattening. |
  • In UpdateStopQuantity, add a dedicated catch for known InvalidOperationException patterns from CreateNewStopOrder/CancelOrder, logging warnings and position risk without throwing.
  • For unexpected exceptions in UpdateStopQuantity, log as critical with full details and avoid rethrowing to allow safety flows to continue.
  • In CreateNewStopOrder, add a catch for known InvalidOperationException from SubmitOrderUnmanaged/CreateOrder and log warnings only.
  • On general exceptions in CreateNewStopOrder, log critical details, attempt an emergency FlattenPositionByName, catch and log failures of that flatten, and do not rethrow.
  • Adjust ValidateStopOrderPreconditions signature to ignore the stopPrice parameter (rename to underscore) to reflect unused semantics while keeping the contract.
| `src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs` | | Make IPC broadcast loops resilient to per-client failures while tightening error classification. |
  • In SendResponseToRemote, split exception handling into System.IO.IOException (network/client disconnect) and general Exception to separately log critical IPC errors per client.
  • On unexpected IPC exceptions, log with client id, mark the client as disconnected, and explicitly avoid throwing to prevent partitioning the broadcast fleet, aligning with Deviation #2.
| `src/V12_002.UI.IPC.Commands.Misc.cs` | | Relax fail-fast behavior in flatten management paths while adding specific handling for known order-change quirks. |
  • In ManageCIT, introduce a catch for known InvalidOperationException from ChangeOrder, logging a warning and continuing, and treat all other exceptions as critical with full ToString logging but without rethrowing.
  • In FlattenAll, add a dedicated catch for known InvalidOperationException patterns in DispatchFleetFlatten/CancelOrder and log as warnings, while logging unexpected exceptions as critical and not rethrowing so callers can continue.
| `src/V12_002.Orders.Management.Flatten.cs` | | Harden lifecycle cleanup to avoid rethrows during disposal while documenting boundary-guard deviation. |
  • In CleanupMmioAndEvents, add a specific catch for ObjectDisposedException when disposing the MMIO mirror and log as a known-shutdown condition.
  • For general exceptions during MMIO disposal, upgrade logging to CRITICAL, document Jane Street Deviation #2 (boundary exception guards must not rethrow), and avoid rethrowing to let subsequent cleanup steps run.
| `src/V12_002.Lifecycle.cs` | ---
Tips and commands #### Interacting with Sourcery - **Trigger a new review:** Comment `@sourcery-ai review` on the pull request. - **Continue discussions:** Reply directly to Sourcery's review comments. - **Generate a GitHub issue from a review comment:** Ask Sourcery to create an issue from a review comment by replying to it. You can also reply to a review comment with `@sourcery-ai issue` to create an issue from it. - **Generate a pull request title:** Write `@sourcery-ai` anywhere in the pull request title to generate a title at any time. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai title` on the pull request to (re-)generate the title at any time. - **Generate a pull request summary:** Write `@sourcery-ai summary` anywhere in the pull request body to generate a PR summary at any time exactly where you want it. You can also comment `@sourcery-ai summary` on the pull request to (re-)generate the summary at any time. - **Generate reviewer's guide:** Comment `@sourcery-ai guide` on the pull request to (re-)generate the reviewer's guide at any time. - **Resolve all Sourcery comments:** Comment `@sourcery-ai resolve` on the pull request to resolve all Sourcery comments. Useful if you've already addressed all the comments and don't want to see them anymore. - **Dismiss all Sourcery reviews:** Comment `@sourcery-ai dismiss` on the pull request to dismiss all existing Sourcery reviews. Especially useful if you want to start fresh with a new review - don't forget to comment `@sourcery-ai review` to trigger a new review! #### Customizing Your Experience Access your [dashboard](https://app.sourcery.ai) to: - Enable or disable review features such as the Sourcery-generated pull request summary, the reviewer's guide, and others. - Change the review language. - Add, remove or edit custom review instructions. - Adjust other review settings. #### Getting Help - [Contact our support team](mailto:support@sourcery.ai) for questions or feedback. - Visit our [documentation](https://docs.sourcery.ai) for detailed guides and information. - Keep in touch with the Sourcery team by following us on [X/Twitter](https://x.com/SourceryAI), [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/sourcery-ai/) or [GitHub](https://github.com/sourcery-ai).

## Walkthrough This PR refines signal documentation and implements defensive exception handling across order management and flatten operations, distinguishing known operation failures from unexpected critical errors while suppressing rethrows to ensure cleanup and continued processing. ## Changes **Exception handling resilience and documentation refinement** |Layer / File(s)|Summary| |---|---| |**Signal broadcaster documentation and comments**
`src/SignalBroadcaster.cs`|XML documentation for signal structs and event declarations simplified to unified "Zero-allocation struct for Jane Street alignment" notes. Broadcast method validation comment wording updated without behavioral changes.| |**Lifecycle cleanup error handling refinement**
`src/V12_002.Lifecycle.cs`|ObjectDisposedException now caught explicitly with targeted logging before generic exception handler, allowing cleanup to continue without interruption.| |**Order management flatten exception filtering**
`src/V12_002.Orders.Management.Flatten.cs`|ManageCIT and FlattenAll add filtered InvalidOperationException catches for known message patterns logged as warnings, while generic catches log critical errors without rethrowing to permit remaining fleet account processing.| |**Stop order sync defensive exception handling**
`src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs`|UpdateStopQuantity and CreateNewStopOrder distinguish known InvalidOperationException patterns (warning logs) from unexpected exceptions (critical logs with emergency flatten fallback). ValidateStopOrderPreconditions marks stopPrice parameter as unused discard.| |**SIMA flatten operation exception resilience**
`src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs`|Five methods (FlattenAllApexAccounts, PumpFlattenOps, ChainNextFlattenOp, EmergencyFlattenSingleFleetAccount, ClosePositionsOnlyApexAccounts) add filtered InvalidOperationException handling for known failures with per-account error containment and queue draining on unexpected exceptions.| |**IPC command response and flatten target error handling**
`src/V12_002.UI.IPC.Commands.Misc.cs`|SendResponseToRemote splits IOException from general Exception to isolate per-client failures. FlattenSpecificTarget now rethrows unexpected exceptions after logging critical error instead of silently swallowing them.| ­ƒÄ» 3 (Moderate) | ÔÅ▒´©Å ~25 minutes

The required threshold is 80.00%. | Write docstrings for the functions missing them to satisfy the coverage threshold. |
Ô£à Passed checks (4 passed) | Check name | Status | Explanation | | :------------------------: | :------- | :----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | Title check | Ô£à Passed | The title accurately describes the main objectiveÔÇöresolving critical V12 DNA violations from PR `#17`ÔÇöand is concise and specific. | | Linked Issues check | Ô£à Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Out of Scope Changes check | Ô£à Passed | Check skipped because no linked issues were found for this pull request. | | Description check | Ô£à Passed | PR description provides comprehensive context including mission scope, file changes, technical justification, validation results, and bot findings, though some template sections are not explicitly filled. |
Ô£Å´©Å Tip: You can configure your own custom pre-merge checks in the settings.

ÔÇó Enhanced IPC broadcast resilience to prevent single-client failures from partitioning fleet

Zero-Allocation"] -->|"Simplified Docs"| B["Clean Comments"] C["Exception Handling"] -->|"Granular Catches"| D["Fail-Fast Guards"] D -->|"No Rethrows"| E["Safety Paths"] F["SIMA Flatten"] -->|"Fallback Logic"| G["Emergency Flatten"] H["IPC Broadcast"] -->|"Client Isolation"| I["Fleet Resilience"] ```

Code Review by Qodo

­ƒÉ× Bugs (2) ­ƒôÿ Rule violations (1)
1. acct.Name logged in Print ­ƒôÿ Rule violation Ôø¿ Security Zero-Trust Protocols (MANDATORY) #3
>
>Description >
> >
>New Print(...) statements output Account.Name values directly, which can expose sensitive fleet
>account identifiers in logs/UI outputs. Compliance requires using BMad aliases (e.g., F01) instead
>of real account names in any external-facing output.
>
>
>
>Code >
> >[src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[R143-145]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/pull/4/files#diff-772f0ba1dd8ec162461ce23b3eb8b633cad8fe9cd9b20c50917769b38eb5754dR143-R145) >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>PR Compliance ID 4 requires that external-facing outputs (including logs) use BMad aliases rather
>than real account names. The changed code prints acct.Name / item.Account.Name directly in
>new/modified Print(...) statements.
>
> > Rule Zero-Trust Protocols (MANDATORY) #3: Obscure Sensitive Account Names Using BMad Aliases in External-Facing Outputs > [src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[143-145]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs/#L143-L145) > [src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[208-215]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs/#L208-L215) > [src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[490-496]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs/#L490-L496) >
>
>Agent prompt >
> >``` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >## Issue description >New log lines print raw `Account.Name` values (e.g., `acct.Name`, `item.Account.Name`) which may leak sensitive fleet identities. Compliance requires replacing these with BMad aliases (e.g., `F01`, `F02`) in external-facing outputs such as logs/`Print(...)`. > >## Issue Context >The codebase already has fleet alias utilities (`BuildFleetAliasMap(...)` and `GetIpcFleetIdentity(...)`) used to avoid exposing real account identities. Apply the same approach (or a log-specific alias helper that never returns the raw name) to the new/modified `Print(...)` lines in SIMA flatten paths. > >## Fix Focus Areas >- src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[143-155] >- src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[208-227] >- src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[490-496] >``` > Ôôÿ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools >
2. Stop failure not surfaced ­ƒÉ× Bug Ôëí Correctness
>
>Description >
> >
>CreateNewStopOrder() now swallows InvalidOperationException during stop submission/creation and only
>logs a warning, then returns normally. This prevents UpdateStopQuantity() from emitting its
>ÔÇ£POSITION MAY BE UNPROTECTEDÔÇØ handling and can leave the position without a protective stop or
>emergency-flatten response on this failure path.
>
>
>
>Code >
> >[src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs[R531-537]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/pull/4/files#diff-b7c79c0370c14728e7192256f4766bf25c0b14e7226018897e1a76e33e36fb1eR531-R537) >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>UpdateStopQuantity() only prints POSITION MAY BE UNPROTECTED when an exception escapes its
>stop-update block; swallowing the exception in CreateNewStopOrder() suppresses that handling. The
>file itself documents that stop submission failures can leave positions unprotected, and the
>emergency-flatten logic is not invoked for the new InvalidOperationException catch path.
>
> > [src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs[431-449]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs/#L431-L449) > [src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs[479-495]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs/#L479-L495) > [src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs[531-559]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs/#L531-L559) > [src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs[663-666]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs/#L663-L666) >
>
>Agent prompt >
> >``` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >## Issue description >`CreateNewStopOrder()` catches `InvalidOperationException` (Submit/Create order ÔÇ£known quirkÔÇØ) and returns after logging a warning. Because it returns normally, `UpdateStopQuantity()` will not hit its catch blocks that print `POSITION MAY BE UNPROTECTED`, and the `CreateNewStopOrder()` InvalidOperationException path also does not attempt emergency flatten. > >## Issue Context >This code path is explicitly safety-critical: the file comments note broker rejection can leave positions unprotected. Today, only `newStop == null` and the general `catch (Exception)` path attempt emergency flatten; the new `InvalidOperationException` catch does not. > >## Fix Focus Areas >- src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs[431-449] >- src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs[454-559] > >## Suggested fix >Choose one of: >1) Treat this `InvalidOperationException` as a hard failure: log `CRITICAL`, print `POSITION MAY BE UNPROTECTED`, and call `FlattenPositionByName(entryName)` (mirroring the general-exception behavior). >2) Re-throw (or wrap and throw) so `UpdateStopQuantity()` can execute its existing unprotected-position logging/handling. >3) Refactor `CreateNewStopOrder()` to return `bool success` and have `UpdateStopQuantity()` explicitly handle `false` by logging/flattening. > >Maintain the ÔÇ£do not crash the fleetÔÇØ intent, but do not allow this specific failure mode to appear as a successful stop update. >``` > Ôôÿ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools >
3. Flatten queue can stall ­ƒÉ× Bug Ôÿ╝ Reliability
>
>Description >
> >
>In SIMA chunked flatten, TriggerCustomEvent scheduling failures in ChainNextFlattenOp() (and also
>the initial kick) clear isFlattenRunning but do not drain or complete _pendingFlattenOps. If
>scheduling fails after some accounts are processed, remaining accounts will not be flattened unless
>a separate future call happens to re-kick the pump.
>
>
>
>Code >
> >[src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[R369-374]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/pull/4/files#diff-772f0ba1dd8ec162461ce23b3eb8b633cad8fe9cd9b20c50917769b38eb5754dR369-R374) >
>
>Evidence >
> >
>PumpFlattenOps() always chains via ChainNextFlattenOp() in finally; when TriggerCustomEvent
>fails there, the function releases the global flatten guard and exits while _pendingFlattenOps may
>still contain work. The file shows all PumpFlattenOps() invocations are scheduled via
>TriggerCustomEvent, so a scheduling failure can strand remaining work until some unrelated future
>action triggers a new scheduling attempt.
>
> > [src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[90-103]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs/#L90-L103) > [src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[183-233]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs/#L183-L233) > [src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[361-388]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs/#L361-L388) > [src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[547-565]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs/#L547-L565) > [src/V12_002.SIMA.Dispatch.cs[157-163]](https://github.com/backtothefutures83-oss/universal-or-strategy/blob/d88b5c5fc55d9cb039a0a75831a2d6c94be964fd/src/V12_002.SIMA.Dispatch.cs/#L157-L163) >
>
>Agent prompt >
> >``` >The issue below was found during a code review. Follow the provided context and guidance below and implement a solution > >## Issue description >`ChainNextFlattenOp()` is responsible for scheduling the next `PumpFlattenOps()` cycle while `_pendingFlattenOps` is non-empty. If `TriggerCustomEvent` throws, the code releases `isFlattenRunning` and returns without draining the queue or performing a fallback flatten, leaving the remaining queued accounts unprocessed. > >The same pattern exists on the initial pump kick in `FlattenAllApexAccounts()` / `ClosePositionsOnlyApexAccounts()` for `InvalidOperationException` TriggerCustomEvent failures: guard is released but the queue remains populated. > >## Issue Context >`PumpFlattenOps()` calls `ChainNextFlattenOp()` in a `finally`, so this affects real mid-flight flattening. Also, `PumpFlattenOps()` is only ever invoked via `TriggerCustomEvent(...PumpFlattenOps...)` call sites; if scheduling fails, there is no internal retry loop. > >## Fix Focus Areas >- src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[90-103] >- src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[173-233] >- src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[361-388] >- src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs[547-565] > >## Suggested fix >On `TriggerCustomEvent` failure (both initial kick and chaining): >- Prefer safety: synchronously drain `_pendingFlattenOps` and execute the same cancel/close steps for each item (similar to the existing fallback in the `catch (Exception)` path in `FlattenAllApexAccounts()`), OR >- Schedule a bounded retry (e.g., re-TriggerCustomEvent once on next tick) while keeping `isFlattenRunning` true until either completion or explicit abandonment. > >If you choose to abandon flatten, explicitly drain/clear `_pendingFlattenOps` and log that flatten did not complete, to avoid silently leaving stale work items and a false sense of completion. >``` > Ôôÿ Copy this prompt and use it to remediate the issue with your preferred AI generation tools >

Code Review ÔÜá´©Å Changes requested 0 resolved / 1 findings Resolves V12 DNA violations by reverting signal types to structs and implementing resilient boundary exception guards. The exception filter in the flattening workflow uses an incorrect method name and will not catch targeted errors.
ÔÜá´©Å Bug: Exception filter uses user method name, will never match ­ƒôä src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs:436-437 The `when` filter at line 437 checks `ex.Message.Contains("CreateNewStopOrder")`, but `CreateNewStopOrder` is your own wrapper method name - it will never appear in an NT8 `InvalidOperationException` message. NT8 throws with API-level names like `SubmitOrderUnmanaged` or `CreateOrder` (as correctly used in the similar filter at line 532). This means the `InvalidOperationException` catch clause for known quirks will never trigger for that condition, and all such exceptions will fall through to the generic `catch (Exception ex)` block instead - which logs as CRITICAL and prints the unprotected warning anyway, but misclassifies the severity.
Use NT8 API method names that actually appear in exception messages, consistent with the filter at line 531-532. ```` catch (InvalidOperationException ex) when (ex.Message.Contains("SubmitOrderUnmanaged") || ex.Message.Contains("CreateOrder") || ex.Message.Contains("CancelOrder")) ````

The PR systematically improves exception handling and documentation across the codebase. All changes follow the stated Jane Street alignment principles and strengthen error isolation to prevent fleet partitioning. The implementation correctly handles edge cases and maintains position safety. No blocking defects identified.

---
You can now have the agent implement changes and create commits directly on your pull request's source branch. Simply comment with /q followed by your request in natural language to ask the agent to make changes.

Hey - I've found 1 issue, and left some high level feedback:

- The new exception filters that rely on `ex.Message.Contains("...")` to detect known NT8 quirks are brittle and culture-dependent; consider matching on more stable signals (specific exception types, error codes/HResult, or a helper that centralizes and documents the matching logic) so behaviors donÔÇÖt change silently on message text changes.
- There is a lot of duplicated error-handling/logging logic (e.g., the various `TriggerCustomEvent`/order-operation `InvalidOperationException` handlers and the critical log patterns in the flatten/stop-sync paths); factoring these into shared helpers would reduce copyÔÇæpaste, keep the Jane Street deviation rules in one place, and make future tweaks to the boundary-guard behavior safer.

## Overall Comments
- The new exception filters that rely on `ex.Message.Contains("...")` to detect known NT8 quirks are brittle and culture-dependent; consider matching on more stable signals (specific exception types, error codes/HResult, or a helper that centralizes and documents the matching logic) so behaviors donÔÇÖt change silently on message text changes.
- There is a lot of duplicated error-handling/logging logic (e.g., the various `TriggerCustomEvent`/order-operation `InvalidOperationException` handlers and the critical log patterns in the flatten/stop-sync paths); factoring these into shared helpers would reduce copyÔÇæpaste, keep the Jane Street deviation rules in one place, and make future tweaks to the boundary-guard behavior safer.

<issue_to_address>
**question (bug_risk):** Behavior change: non-InvalidOperationException errors in FlattenSpecificTarget now rethrow instead of being logged and swallowed.

The prior `catch (Exception ex)` swallowed all errors from `FlattenSpecificTarget`, whereas now only `InvalidOperationException` is logged and everything else is rethrown as CRITICAL. This may introduce new unhandled exceptions or aborts for existing callers (especially IPC paths) that assumed failures were logged but not propagated. Please verify that all call sites can tolerate this new fail-fast behavior, or consider restricting which exception types are allowed to bubble up.
</issue_to_address>
~~~

</details>

Inline comments:
In `@src/V12_002.Lifecycle.cs`:
- Around line 177-181: Change the log prefix used in the ObjectDisposedException
catch for the MMIO mirror so it reflects an expected shutdown condition rather
than an error: in the catch (ObjectDisposedException ex) block where
Print("[SHUTDOWN_ERROR] MMIO mirror already disposed: " + ex.Message) is called,
replace the "[SHUTDOWN_ERROR]" prefix with a calmer label such as
"[SHUTDOWN_INFO]" or "[SHUTDOWN_NOTICE]" so Print(...) reports an
informational/notice-level message for this expected disposal event.

In `@src/V12_002.Orders.Management.Flatten.cs`:
- Around line 220-228: The current catch in FlattenAll is too broad and will
abort the remaining phases if CancelMasterEntryOrders() or
DispatchFleetFlatten() throws the filtered InvalidOperationException; narrow the
scope so the known-quirk catch only surrounds the specific calls that can throw
(e.g., wrap DispatchFleetFlatten() and/or CancelMasterEntryOrders() in their own
try/catch) and in those local catches log the warning and continue, ensuring
ResetSyncStateAndPurgeFollowers(), FlattenFilledMasterPositions(), and
CancelUnfilledMasterEntries() always run; update FlattenAll so only the specific
failing call is caught with the ex.Message filter and other exceptions still
propagate or are handled separately.

In `@src/V12_002.Orders.Management.StopSync.cs`:
- Around line 531-558: The InvalidOperationException branch for
CreateNewStopOrder currently just logs and returns, leaving the position
unprotected; change it to perform the same emergency-flatten fallback as the
generic catch: after the warning Print(...) call in the catch
(InvalidOperationException ex) when (...) block, remove any early return and
invoke FlattenPositionByName(entryName) inside a try/catch, logging
success/failure using the same messages/format as the generic catch (use
flatEx.ToString() on failure) and do not rethrow so the safety fallback runs
consistently for both exception paths.

In `@src/V12_002.SIMA.Flatten.cs`:
- Around line 97-102: The catch blocks that handle TriggerCustomEvent failures
(the one releasing isFlattenRunning and printing a warning) must also drain or
clear the shared queue _pendingFlattenOps to avoid replaying stale ops; update
those catch handlers to call the same drain/fallback routine used by
FlattenAllApexAccounts (or explicitly flush _pendingFlattenOps) before
returning, ensuring you maintain existing synchronization (set
isFlattenRunning=false only after draining) and preserve the logging via Print
so double-processing/stale items cannot be processed on the next run.

In `@src/V12_002.UI.IPC.Commands.Misc.cs`:
- Around line 318-327: The current catch blocks around the entire foreach in
FlattenSpecificTarget cause one InvalidOperationException to abort processing of
all remaining positions; move the InvalidOperationException handling inside the
loop (wrap the per-position processing in a try/catch) so a single bad entry
logs via Print("ERROR FlattenSpecificTarget: " + ex.Message) and continues to
the next position, while leaving the broader Exception (CRITICAL) behavior as-is
(either outside the loop to still fail-fast for unexpected errors or rethrow as

Prompt for AI agents (unresolved issues) ```text Check if these issues are valid ÔÇö if so, understand the root cause of each and fix them. If appropriate, use sub-agents to investigate and fix each issue separately. P2: The `InvalidOperationException` catch (the "known NT8 quirk") abandons all enqueued fleet accounts without attempting fallback flatten. The generic `catch (Exception)` immediately below has a full drain+fallback mechanism. Since this is described as a known/likely failure mode, it arguably needs the same position-protection fallback. P1: Comment says "remaining fleet accounts still need flattening" but no fallback flatten is attempted ÔÇö items left in `_pendingFlattenOps` are silently abandoned. Consider draining the queue and performing synchronous fallback flatten (matching the pattern in `FlattenAllApexAccounts`'s generic catch), or at minimum logging how many accounts were abandoned. P1: Releasing `isFlattenRunning` here without draining or retrying `_pendingFlattenOps` can strand queued flatten work after scheduling failures. Drain/clear the queue or perform a bounded retry before returning. P1: Inconsistency with `FlattenAllApexAccounts`: on unexpected exception, queued position-close operations are silently abandoned. `FlattenAllApexAccounts` properly drains the queue and performs synchronous fallback, but this method just releases the guard. In a safety-critical path, this could leave positions open. P1: Re-throwing in a flatten loop can leave remaining positions unflattened. If an unexpected error occurs while flattening one position, the `throw` aborts the entire loopÔÇöpotentially leaving other positions open in a safety-critical operation. This contradicts the "boundary isolation" principle applied to `SendResponseToRemote` above ("one bad client must not partition fleet"). Consider swallowing the exception and continuing to the next position, or moving the try-catch inside the loop. P0: Catching this known InvalidOperationException at the outer `FlattenAll` scope aborts the rest of the flatten pipeline. Handle the quirk at the specific failing call so sync reset, position flatten, and entry cancellation still execute. P0: This branch logs and returns when stop creation fails, which can leave the position unprotected without triggering emergency flatten or upstream unprotected-position handling. ```
